steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Two independent clauses meet at the blank: "Scholars... needn't despair" (IC) and "they can still procure a copy..." (IC). The transition word "though" attaches to the first clause. To join two ICs, you need a semicolon (or period, or comma + FANBOYS conjunction).

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
(C) "despair, though;" sets off "though" with a comma inside the first clause, then a semicolon joins the two ICs.

(A) and (B) are comma splices. (D)'s em dash–no-second-half-punctuation doesn't correctly bound a parenthetical "though."

That matches choice C — C is correct.
